name: Full Body Foundation
description: Two alternating whole-body days, three sessions a week (A/B/A, then B/A/B). Every session hits squat, push, pull, and hinge patterns — the fastest way to build a base.

name: Upper / Lower Split
description: Four days a week: two upper and two lower sessions with complementary A/B emphases — horizontal vs vertical pressing up top, squat-led vs hip-led below.

name: Push Pull Legs
description: The classic three-day rotation — push, pull, legs. Run it once through for 3 days a week or twice for 6.

name: Dumbbell Home Builder
description: Two alternating full-body dumbbell days for home or apartment gyms — nothing but a pair of dumbbells required.

name: Machine Fundamentals
description: A joint-friendly upper/lower pair built on machines and cables — ideal for learning movement patterns with guided resistance.

name: Hybrid Engine
description: Strength plus aerobic base: two lift-then-zone-2 sessions and two easy run/walk days a week keep you strong without losing your engine.

name: Bodyweight Travel Kit
description: One superset-driven bodyweight session you can run anywhere, three times a week — enough stimulus to hold muscle on the road.
